question: Spread: Tunisia (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Tunisia" if Tunisia win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Haiti".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Haiti vs. Tunisia - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 8:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tunisia enters as clear favorites against Haiti in this international friendly, with trader consensus reflecting their superior FIFA ranking (28th vs. Haiti's 83rd) and recent form, including a 1-0 win over Equatorial Guinea. Haiti's attack hinges on Duckens Nazon, but defensive vulnerabilities exposed in a 3-1 loss to Saudi Arabia could prove costly. No major injuries reported from official team updates, though Tunisia benefits from rest after a lighter schedule. Upcoming factors include Haiti's home crowd boost in Port-au-Prince and potential weather disruptions, yet historical head-to-heads favor African sides in neutral-site clashes, tempering upset potential amid the wisdom of crowds pricing.

events.title: Morocco vs. Ecuador - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 4:15 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Morocco enters as the clear trader favorite against Ecuador in this international friendly, with market odds reflecting their strong momentum from a semifinal run at the 2022 World Cup and Africa Cup of Nations final appearance, contrasting Ecuador's inconsistent CONMEBOL qualifying campaign. Recent developments show no major injuries reported for Morocco's key Atlas Lions like Hakim Ziyech or Sofyan Amrabat, while Ecuador misses creative midfielder Moises Caicedo amid Chelsea duties. Head-to-head history favors Morocco's 2-1 group stage victory in Qatar. Traders weigh Morocco's home-soil defensive edge and rest advantages for Europe-based stars against Ecuador's counterattacking threat, with implied probabilities underscoring upset potential in unpredictable friendlies.

events.title: Netherlands vs. Norway - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 3:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Netherlands holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their UEFA Women's Nations League League A home matchup against Norway on October 29 in Enschede, with implied probabilities reflecting about 55-60% win chance amid strong recent form. Norway remains dangerous, topping the group on goal difference after a 4-1 rout of Finland, powered by Ada Hegerberg, but Caroline Graham Hansen's confirmed hamstring absence weakens their wings. Traders eye Netherlands' attacking momentum against Norway's resilient defense, with playoff implications amplifying stakes in this pivotal clash.

The Oranje lead Group 1 unbeaten, fresh off a 1-0 victory over Italy and 2-1 win vs. Finland, boosted by Vivianne Miedema's return from injury and home-field edge at FC Twente Stadion. Norway remains dangerous, topping the group on goal difference after a 4-1 rout of Finland, powered by Ada Hegerberg, but Caroline Graham Hansen's confirmed hamstring absence weakens their wings. Traders eye Netherlands' attacking momentum against Norway's resilient defense, with playoff implications amplifying stakes in this pivotal clash.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany holds a commanding edge in trader sentiment for the Switzerland vs. Germany matchup, with implied probabilities reflecting about 70% chance of a German win based on their superior Nations League form and home advantage at MHPArena. Recent developments include Germany's 5-0 thrashing of Bosnia boosting momentum, while Switzerland struggled in a 1-1 draw with Denmark amid midfield injuries to Xhaka and Freuler per official reports. Head-to-head history favors Germany (4 wins in last 6), and their full-strength attack featuring Havertz and Gnabry contrasts Switzerland's depleted defense. Traders eye rest disparity—Germany fresher post-international break—as key to potential clean-sheet outcome. Upsets remain possible given Switzerland's counterattacking threat.

question: Spread: Switzerland (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 3:45 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Switzerland" if Switzerland win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Germany".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Algeria vs. Guatemala - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 3:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Algeria enters as a heavy favorite against Guatemala in this international friendly, with trader consensus implying over 80% win probability for the North Africans due to their FIFA ranking (46th vs. Guatemala's 111th) and recent form, including a 5-1 thrashing of Rwanda and unbeaten streak in five matches. Key factors include Algeria's attacking depth led by Amine Gouiri and rest advantages from a lighter schedule, while Guatemala struggles with defensive frailties, conceding 2+ goals in four of their last five outings amid CONCACAF Nations League woes. No major injuries reported from official team updates, but watch for lineup rotations as both test squads ahead of World Cup qualifiers; historical head-to-head favors Algeria 4-0 in 2022. Upsets remain possible in friendlies.

events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the Greece vs. Paraguay matchup leans toward a low-scoring affair, with under markets gaining traction amid both teams' defensive setups in international friendlies. Greece, ranked 52nd by FIFA, enters on a winless streak in their last five outings, hampered by injuries to key midfielder Giorgos Giakoumakis and doubts over Vangelis Pavlidis' fitness per recent national team reports. Head-to-head history is sparse, but Paraguay's counterattacking style exploits Greece's home vulnerabilities, where they've conceded in 70% of recent games. Upcoming rest advantages and neutral venue tilt implied probabilities toward Paraguay covering or draw, reflecting crowd wisdom on form disparities.

events.title: Montenegro vs. Andorra - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 27 at 1:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Montenegro holds a commanding edge over Andorra in their UEFA Nations League C clash, with trader consensus pricing a Montenegro victory at over 90% implied probability due to their superior FIFA ranking (72nd vs. Andorra's 173rd) and historical dominance—winning both prior meetings 4-0 and 2-0. question: Will there be no Head of State in Iran end of 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to the individual who de facto holds and exercises the powers of the head of state of the Islamic Republic of Iran on December 31, 2026 at 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, “de facto holds” refers to the individual who exercises primary governing authority over the Iranian state at that time, including effective control over the armed forces, national institutions, and core executive decision-making, regardless of formal title, constitutional designation, or international recognition.

Formal appointment, recognition by the United Nations, or recognition by foreign governments is not required.

If more than one individual claims to be head of state, this market will resolve to the individual who demonstrably exercises primary governing control within Iran’s territory at the specified time.

Indicators of de facto authority may include control over the armed forces and security services; control over executive ministries and state administrative institutions; enforcement of national laws; issuance of binding national directives; and effective control over the capital and core state infrastructure.

Symbolic status, foreign recognition without domestic control, nomination without effective authority, or expired prior service will not qualify.

If no individual exercises effective governing control at the specified time, this market will resolve to “No Head of State”.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting assessing who exercises effective governing authority at the specified time.
